takeaways: Fed holds rates at 3.5%-3.75% with only one cut projected for 2026 — CRE borrowing costs stay elevated as 10-Year Treasury hovers near 4.26%, keeping cap rate compression gradual.
Senior housing occupancy nears 89% with new supply at record lows since 2006 — operators have rare pricing power as 10,000+ Americans turn 65 daily and construction pipelines remain thin.
Proptech VC funding surged 176% YoY in January 2026 to $1.7B as agentic AI moves from pilot to production — four new AI-native proptech unicorns have emerged since mid-2025.

items.headline: Fed Holds Rates Steady, Signals Only One Cut in 2026
items.summary: The FOMC voted 11-1 on March 18 to keep the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75%, raising its 2026 inflation forecast to 2.7% PCE. The dot plot still projects one cut this year and another in 2027, but timing remains uncertain amid elevated economic uncertainty. For senior housing operators, higher-for-longer rates mean continued pressure on acquisition financing and development costs.

items.headline: Senior Housing Occupancy Hits 89% as New Supply Falls to Record Low
items.summary: NIC MAP data shows occupancy reached 88.7% in Q3 2025, the 17th consecutive quarter of improvement, with independent living surpassing 90% for the first time since 2019. Fewer than 1,500 new units were added in Primary Markets — just 0.7% YoY growth, the lowest since NIC began tracking in 2006. Industry-wide occupancy is projected to exceed 90% by year-end 2026.

items.headline: 86% of Investors Plan to Increase Senior Housing Exposure in 2026
items.summary: JLL survey data reveals overwhelming investor appetite for senior housing, with 86% planning to grow their sector allocation this year. Discounted pricing of 30-50% off 2019 peaks across price points and regions is drawing capital back in, while average monthly rents have climbed to $5,479 with annual rent growth exceeding 4%.

items.headline: Agentic AI Reshapes CRE as Proptech VC Hits $1.7B in January Alone
items.summary: Venture capital firms invested $1.7B in proptech in January 2026, a 176% YoY increase. Four AI-native proptech startups have reached unicorn status since mid-2025, all automating core real estate processes. Agentic AI is moving beyond content generation to autonomous decision-making in procurement, pricing, and portfolio management.

items.headline: CRE Capital Markets Enter Recovery Phase with Abundant Liquidity
items.summary: Commercial mortgage rates start at 5.39% as of March 27, with conventional rates ranging 4.99%-8.75%. CBRE projects cap rates for most property types to compress 5-15 basis points in 2026. The rapid repricing cycle from aggressive monetary tightening appears largely complete, though valuation recovery remains sensitive to long-term rate movements.

items.headline: Senior Living Construction Cycle Stretches to 29 Months, Delaying Relief
items.summary: Even as construction starts tick up in 2026, the average build cycle has stretched to 29 months, meaning new projects won't deliver until 2028 at earliest. Developers face stubborn costs pushing capital toward strong operators and disciplined execution. Active starts include projects by Erickson, Harbor Retirement Associates, and Charter Senior Living across Florida, Maryland, and Texas.

items.headline: 44% of CRE Firms Distrust AI-Generated Analysis, Slowing Adoption
items.summary: Despite surging proptech investment, trust remains the primary barrier to deeper AI adoption in high-value real estate workflows. Nearly half of firms say their investment committees distrust AI-generated analysis, creating a gap between early adopters gaining operational advantages and laggards. Industry leaders emphasize building AI on strong data foundations integrated into mission-critical systems.

items.headline: Real Estate Agents Report AI Reshaping Daily Workflows, Not Replacing Jobs
items.summary: An Inman survey of real estate agents finds AI is rapidly reshaping how they work and raising expectations industry-wide. High-volume teams and solo agents alike are using AI to automate repeatable, non-client-facing tasks — freeing time for relationship building. The consensus: AI augments rather than replaces, but agents who ignore it risk falling behind.

items.headline: NAIOP Report Details Shifting CRE Financing Landscape for 2026
items.summary: A new NAIOP report outlines U.S. CRE financing trends showing abundant liquidity even as default rates increase for obsolete assets. The lending environment remains healthy overall, with capital flowing again and leasing fundamentals generally stabilizing. Good-quality assets are expected to see greater cap rate compression than the market average.

items.headline: HBR Study Warns Leaders About 'Trendslop' in AI Strategic Advice
items.summary: Harvard Business Review research published March 16 finds that leading LLMs consistently recommend strategies aligned with managerial buzzwords rather than context-specific logic — a bias dubbed 'trendslop.' When prompted on seven key strategy questions, AI recommended the same trendy solutions regardless of context. Leaders should use AI to expand options, not make final strategic choices.

items.headline: CEO Ownership of AI Strategy Doubles as Half Say Their Job Is on the Line
items.summary: Nearly 75% of CEOs now say they are their organization's main decision-maker on AI, double from a year ago. Half believe their position depends on AI delivering ROI. Companies plan to double AI spending in 2026, making AI governance and execution a C-suite imperative rather than a technology department initiative.

items.headline: 71% of Leaders Under Increased Stress as U.S. Layoffs Hit 10-Year High
items.summary: Organizations enter 2026 lean after U.S. layoffs surged to the highest point in a decade. While restructuring and automation have streamlined teams, workloads haven't decreased — 71% of leaders report increased stress and 40% are considering leaving their roles. The trend underscores the importance of leadership wellness and retention strategies for operators scaling amid tight labor markets.

items.headline: McKinsey Identifies Three Critical Organizational Decisions for 2026
items.summary: McKinsey's State of Organizations 2026 research highlights three urgent decisions leaders must make now: how to integrate AI into core operations, how to redesign work for hybrid teams, and how to build adaptive talent pipelines. The report emphasizes that strategy must become a continuous learning system rather than an annual planning exercise.

items.headline: Gentry Park Orlando Holds Grand Reveal Open House
items.summary: Meridian Senior Living's Gentry Park community in Orlando held its Grand Reveal Open House on March 28, 2026, showcasing newly updated spaces and inviting prospective residents to experience daily life in the community. The event reflects continued operator investment in property upgrades to attract residents in a competitive market with rising occupancy.

items.headline: Global Wellness Summit Declares 2026 the Year of 'Emotional Fitness'
items.summary: The Global Wellness Summit's 2026 trends report introduces emotional fitness as a new health category, emphasizing cognitive performance and emotional resilience. Corporate wellness is shifting from isolated programs to complete well-being ecosystems. For senior housing operators, the trend signals growing resident expectations for holistic wellness programming beyond basic fitness amenities.

items.headline: Personalized Wellness Goes Mainstream with AI-Driven Health Tracking
items.summary: 2026 marks a turning point for personalized wellness, powered by AI analytics, at-home biomarker tests, and advanced wearable devices tracking more than steps and sleep. The longevity movement is expanding into real estate design, with homes becoming wellness hubs. Senior living communities that integrate personalized health tech could gain a competitive edge in attracting health-conscious boomers.

items.headline: Longevity Real Estate Emerges as New Asset Class
items.summary: Longevity is no longer just a service — it's becoming a lifestyle built into where people live. The wellness-real estate convergence is creating a new asset class as developers integrate health-focused design, air quality systems, and biophilic elements into residential communities. The trend has direct implications for senior housing operators designing next-generation communities.

items.headline: Smart Bricks Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Automate Real Estate Investing
items.summary: Smart Bricks closed a $5 million pre-seed round to build an AI platform automating real estate investment analysis and execution. The startup joins a wave of AI-native proptech firms attracting early-stage capital, reflecting investor confidence that agentic AI can streamline traditionally manual investment workflows in commercial and residential real estate.
